intitule: Reading Comprehension: Socialising with Neighbours of Other Religions

enonce.texte:
Read the text carefully and answer the questions that follow.

In our neighbourhood, we have people from different religious backgrounds. There are Christians, Muslims, and followers of traditional African religions. Despite these differences, we live together in harmony. Every year, we celebrate the end of Ramadan with our Muslim neighbours. They invite us to share meals and exchange gifts. During Christmas, we invite them to our homes. My best friend, Ahmed, is a Muslim. We play football together, help each other with homework, and share our meals. His mother taught me how to prepare delicious African dishes, and my mother taught her how to bake cakes. We have learned to respect each other's beliefs and traditions. Our parents always say that religion should not divide us because we are all human beings. We have also learned to use ellipsis in our conversations to make our speech more natural. For example, when someone says, "Ahmed loves football, and so does Jean," we understand that both share the same interest.
images: A diverse group of children from different religious backgrounds playing together happily in a neighbourhood park, with a mosque and a church visible in the background.

questions.texte: What is the main idea of the text?
questions.reponse: The text is about people from different religious backgrounds living together in harmony and respecting each other's beliefs.

questions.texte: Identify two religious groups mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: Christians and Muslims are mentioned. Traditional African religions are also mentioned.

questions.texte: Explain how the neighbours celebrate their religious festivals together.
questions.reponse: They celebrate the end of Ramadan with their Muslim neighbours by sharing meals and exchanging gifts. During Christmas, they invite their Muslim neighbours to their homes.

questions.texte: Analyse the relationship between the narrator and Ahmed.
questions.reponse: They are best friends who play football together, help each other with homework, share meals, and learn from each other's families.

questions.texte: What lesson do the parents teach their children according to the text?
questions.reponse: Parents teach that religion should not divide people because we are all human beings.

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following sentences using ellipsis (e.g., 'so does', 'neither does', etc.).

questions.texte: Marie loves to read books. John also loves to read books.
questions.reponse: Marie loves to read books, and so does John.

questions.texte: Peter does not like spicy food. Mary does not like spicy food either.
questions.reponse: Peter does not like spicy food, and neither does Mary.

questions.texte: She will attend the meeting. Her brother will also attend the meeting.
questions.reponse: She will attend the meeting, and so will her brother.

questions.texte: I have never visited that city. My friend has never visited that city either.
questions.reponse: I have never visited that city, and neither has my friend.

questions.texte: He can speak French fluently. She can also speak French fluently.
questions.reponse: He can speak French fluently, and so can she.

questions.texte: We shouldn't waste time. They shouldn't waste time either.
questions.reponse: We shouldn't waste time, and neither should they.

intitule: Writing a Short Story about Socialising with Others

enonce.texte: Write a short story (about 150-200 words) about socialising with neighbours of other religions. Use at least three examples of ellipsis in your story.

questions.reponse: Sample answer: Growing up in a diverse community, I learned to appreciate people from different religions. My neighbour, Fatima, is a Muslim, and I am a Christian. Despite our differences, we have become the best of friends. Fatima loves to cook traditional dishes, and so does my mother. They often exchange recipes and cooking tips. During Ramadan, we would join Fatima's family for iftar, and she would come to our house for Christmas dinner. One day, a new family moved in next door. They were afraid to interact with us because of our different religions. Fatima and I decided to welcome them. We invited them for tea, and they accepted. Soon, we all became good friends. The new family did not know our customs, and neither did we know theirs. But we learned together. We celebrated each other's festivals and shared our traditions. My father always says that religion should not divide us, and so does Fatima's father. We are all human beings with the same needs and desires. This experience taught me that socialising with people of other religions enriches our lives.

intitule: Dialogue: Socialising with Neighbours of Other Religions

enonce.texte: Complete the following dialogue between two neighbours from different religions. Use appropriate expressions and ellipsis where possible.

questions.texte:
A: Good morning, Mrs. Amina! How are you today?
B: Good morning, Mrs. Marie! I'm fine, thank you. And you?
A: I'm doing well. I wanted to invite you and your family to our Christmas dinner next week.
B: That's very kind of you! We would love to come. _______ (1) my husband loves to spend time with your family.
A: Wonderful! And we are also looking forward to the end of Ramadan celebration with you.
B: We will be happy to have you. My children are excited to share their traditional dishes with you.
A: My children love your cooking, and _______ (2).
B: That's lovely! You know, my daughter has never tried baking cakes before.
A: She can come over this weekend and learn with my daughter. My daughter loves baking, and _______ (3).
B: Thank you so much! We are so blessed to have neighbours like you.
A: The feeling is mutual, Mrs. Amina. We believe that _______ (4) religion should not divide us.
questions.reponse: 1. and so does / 2. so do they / 3. so does yours / 4. neither

intitule: Understanding and Using Ellipsis in Context

enonce.texte: Read the sentences and choose the correct ellipsis form to complete each one.

questions.texte: Sarah enjoys swimming, and _______ her brother.
I don't like coffee, and _______ my husband.
They have finished their homework, and _______ we.
She wasn't at the party, and _______ her sister.
We will visit the museum tomorrow, and _______ our friends.
He can't speak Spanish, and _______ his wife.
